title: auth — authorization policies
description: Three policies. `AuthPolicy` covers rare auth-surface abilities that need policy gates (most auth flows are self-service and don't policy-check). `SessionPolicy` covers session-management surface — tenant users can view + revoke their own sessions; platform admins can view + revoke any session. `CrossAppGrantPolicy` covers grant issuance + exchange — always self-scoped (the caller's own Identity).

abilities.scope: Always allowed for authenticated users — no permission check. Rate-limited by auth.lockout.password_reset_per_hour.
Always allowed for authenticated users — debounced 60s per identity.
List the caller's own sessions across their Applications. Cross-User listing requires the platform_admin guard's `platform.auth.sessions.viewAny`.
Show detail on the caller's own session. Refuses when the session's tokenable is not the caller's User.
Revoke the caller's own session. Fires SessionRevoked(reason=self_service). Refuses revoking the CURRENT session via this endpoint (must use /auth/logout).
Platform-security listing. Sessions filtered by User / tenant / application / date range. Redacted — no PAT plaintext.
Support-driven session revocation. Fires SessionRevoked(reason=admin_revocation) + notifies the target User's email.
Bulk revoke every session for a User or Identity. Fires AllSessionsRevoked. Used for breach response.
Issue a grant on the caller's own Identity. Refuses if caller lacks a User row on the target Application. Enforces cross_app_sso entitlement on the caller's current tenant.
Exchange a grant on the target Application. No permission check — possession of the grant token IS the auth. Refuses if grant is expired / consumed / issued for a different Identity.

boundary_note: Every ability in this file respects the two-audience guard boundary. Tenant-guard abilities are always scoped to the caller's own Identity / User (`.own`). Platform-guard abilities are cross-user by definition — no `.own` suffix, no tenant scope. Neither guard's abilities leak into the other's policy map — cross-guard authorization checks return false silently.
